latestCommit.message:
fix: restore default scrollback to 10000 and add normalization (#34)

Commit 95bd715 reduced xterm.js scrollback from 10000 to 500 lines to
optimize memory, causing long output (e.g. `seq 1 20000`) to become
unreachable after ~590 lines. Saved settings persisted the regressed
value, blocking any future default from taking effect.

- Restore DEFAULT_TERMINAL_SCROLLBACK to 10000 with named constants
  (MIN=1000, MAX=50000) replacing magic numbers
- Add normalizeScrollbackLines() to clamp invalid values and migrate
  stored 500-line settings back to the default on load
- Normalize scrollback in load/save/getTerminalOptions/getThemeAwareTerminalOptions
  for defense in depth
- Apply term.options.scrollback in-place on appearance change without
  recreating the terminal
- Cap settings slider at MAX_TERMINAL_SCROLLBACK (50000) instead of 100000
- Add regression test writing 20000 lines to real xterm.js Terminal
- Update stale PtyTerminal test mocks for current dependencies
latestCommit.author: htazq

  recreating the terminal
- Cap settings slider at MAX_TERMINAL_SCROLLBACK (50000) instead of 100000
- Add regression test writing 20000 lines to real xterm.js Terminal
- Update stale PtyTerminal test mocks for current dependencies

latestCommit.message:
Expose WebDAV storage channels in the frontend

The backend PR adds WebDAV as a first-class storage channel, so the frontend needs matching controls for selecting, configuring, filtering, and labeling that channel. This follows the existing Telegram/R2/S3/Discord/HuggingFace channel management patterns without adding dependencies.

Constraint: Keep the UI aligned with the existing Element Plus channel management surface

Rejected: Only add the upload dialog radio option | leaves runtime WebDAV channel config inaccessible from system settings

Confidence: high

Scope-risk: moderate

Tested: npm run build; npm test; git diff --check

Not-tested: Manual browser workflow against a live WebDAV server
latestCommit.author: htazq

latest.title: Expose WebDAV storage channels in the frontend

repositories.name: CloudFlare-ImgBed

latestCommit.message:
Keep WebDAV secrets out of file metadata

WebDAV upload records now store only the channel identity and object/public paths. Read, delete, move, and rename operations resolve credentials and headers from the current WebDAV channel config by ChannelName, with a legacy metadata fallback so already-created records remain usable.

Constraint: Preserve existing WebDAV object lifecycle behavior while reducing metadata exposure

Rejected: Keep credentials on every file record | expands exposure through metadata reads, backups, and logs

Confidence: high

Scope-risk: moderate

Directive: Keep WebDAV credentials in upload config/env; do not reintroduce per-file credential metadata

Tested: npm test; node deploy/worker/generate-routes.js; npx wrangler deploy --dry-run --config deploy/worker/wrangler.toml; git diff --check

Not-tested: Live WebDAV provider move/delete/read workflow
latestCommit.author: htazq

latest.title: Enable third-party WebDAV storage without new runtime dependencies
